siteDescription: A production-ready monorepo with Next.js, Sanity CMS, and Cloudflare Workers.
footer: Edit this site in Sanity Studio — content, navigation, and pages are all managed from the CMS.

seo.title: Sanity Starter
seo.description: Next.js + Sanity + Cloudflare — a production-ready starter with Visual Editing and a page builder.

pageBuilder.headline: Build content-driven sites with Sanity + Next.js
pageBuilder.subheadline: This homepage is built from Sanity page builder blocks. Edit the copy, reorder sections, or add new blocks — changes appear on the site after you publish.

pageBuilder.headline: What's included
pageBuilder.subheadline: Everything you need to ship a content site — no boilerplate archaeology required.

features.title: Next.js App Router
features.description: Server components, draft mode, and Live Content API wired up out of the box.

features.title: Sanity Visual Editing
features.description: Presentation Tool previews with click-to-edit overlays when draft mode is on.

features.title: Page builder
features.description: Composable sections — hero, features, steps, and CTA blocks you can mix on any page.

features.title: Cloudflare Workers
features.description: Edge deployment via OpenNext. One command to build and ship.

features.title: Blog ready
features.description: Portable Text blog with starter guides — full setup at /posts/welcome, Mux video at /posts/mux-video-setup.

features.title: Password gates
features.description: Site-wide staging gate via env vars, plus per-page protection managed in the CMS.

pageBuilder.headline: Set up a new project in four steps
pageBuilder.subheadline: Follow these once after cloning the repo. Your content will replace this starter page.

steps.title: Configure environment variables
steps.description: Copy the example env files and add your Sanity project ID, read token, and Mux credentials (Studio only).
steps.code:
cp frontend/.env.example frontend/.env.local
cp studio/.env.example studio/.env.local

steps.title: Install dependencies
steps.description: From the repo root, install all workspace packages.

steps.title: Import starter content
steps.description: Log in to the Sanity CLI, then import this sample dataset. You can re-run this anytime to reset content.

steps.title: Run locally
steps.description: Starts the Next.js frontend and Sanity Studio in parallel.

pageBuilder.headline: Ready to make it yours?
pageBuilder.subheadline: Open Studio to edit this page, add new documents, or remove these starter sections entirely.

title: Guides
description: Step-by-step setup guides for this starter template.

title: Setting up Mux video
excerpt: Mux API tokens, Studio environment variables, uploading video, and how the frontend renders playback without Mux secrets.

children.text: Video never lives in the Sanity asset pipeline. Raw uploads would bypass transcoding, adaptive bitrate, and efficient delivery. Instead, this starter sends video to Mux via sanity-plugin-mux-input and renders it on the frontend with @mux/mux-player-react.
1. Create a Mux account and API token
Sign up at dashboard.mux.com — the free tier includes encoding credits. Then create an API access token:
Mux Dashboard → Settings → API Access Tokens → Generate new token
Enable Mux Video Read and Write permissions
Copy the Token ID and Token Secret (the secret is only shown once)
2. Add credentials to Studio
Mux tokens belong in studio/.env.local only. They are used when editors upload video — not at build time or on the frontend.

# studio/.env.local
SANITY_STUDIO_MUX_TOKEN_ID="your-mux-token-id"
SANITY_STUDIO_MUX_TOKEN_SECRET="your-mux-token-secret"
Restart the Studio dev server after saving. Without these variables, the Mux uploader will not appear.
3. Upload and publish
Open a page with a Hero section (or any field using the media primitive)
Set Media type to Video
Upload a file — Studio sends it to Mux and stores the playback ID in your document
Publish. The unified Media component reads playbackId and renders MuxPlayer on the site
4. Frontend rendering

No Mux env vars are needed in frontend/.env.local. Public videos play using the playbackId stored in Sanity. The Media component also respects aspect ratio overrides and optional parallax from the CMS.
Full environment variable reference
Studio

SANITY_STUDIO_PROJECT_ID="your-project-id"
SANITY_STUDIO_DATASET="production"
SANITY_STUDIO_PREVIEW_URL="http://localhost:3000"
SANITY_STUDIO_MUX_TOKEN_ID="your-mux-token-id"
SANITY_STUDIO_MUX_TOKEN_SECRET="your-mux-token-secret"
Frontend

NEXT_PUBLIC_SANITY_PROJECT_ID="your-project-id"
NEXT_PUBLIC_SANITY_DATASET="production"
SANITY_API_READ_TOKEN="your-read-token"
NEXT_PUBLIC_SANITY_STUDIO_URL="http://localhost:3333"
For signed URLs, DRM, or private playback you would add Mux signing keys on the frontend — that is outside this starter's scope.
Deployed Studio

When you run sanity deploy, add Mux token variables in Sanity Manage → Project → Settings → Environment variables. Do not put Mux secrets in Cloudflare Workers — they are only needed where uploads happen (Studio).
Back to the main starter guide:
Welcome to your Sanity Starter

seo.title: Setting up Mux video — Sanity Starter
seo.description: Connect Mux to Sanity Studio and add video to page builder media fields.

title: Welcome to your Sanity Starter
excerpt: Clone, configure env vars, import sample content, and understand what ships with this Next.js + Sanity + Cloudflare monorepo.

children.text: This starter is a production-ready monorepo: Next.js 15 on the frontend, Sanity Studio for content, and Cloudflare Workers for deployment. Everything on this blog — including this post — is managed in Studio and rendered with Portable Text.
Quick start
After cloning the repo and creating a Sanity project, configure env vars, import this sample content, and start both dev servers:

cp frontend/.env.example frontend/.env.local
cp studio/.env.example studio/.env.local
# Fill in your Sanity project ID and API read token

pnpm install
cd studio && npx sanity login
cd .. && pnpm import-sample-data
pnpm dev
Frontend runs at localhost:3000. Studio runs at localhost:3333. Re-run pnpm import-sample-data anytime to reset all starter content.
Environment variables
Frontend — frontend/.env.local

NEXT_PUBLIC_SANITY_PROJECT_ID="your-project-id"
NEXT_PUBLIC_SANITY_DATASET="production"
SANITY_API_READ_TOKEN="your-read-token"

# Optional
NEXT_PUBLIC_SANITY_STUDIO_URL="http://localhost:3333"
SITE_PROTECTION_ENABLED="true"
SITE_PASSWORD="your-site-password"
Create a read token at sanity.io/manage → API → Tokens (Viewer role). The site-wide password gate is optional — useful for staging.
Studio — studio/.env.local

SANITY_STUDIO_PROJECT_ID="your-project-id"
SANITY_STUDIO_DATASET="production"
SANITY_STUDIO_PREVIEW_URL="http://localhost:3000"

# Required for video uploads
SANITY_STUDIO_MUX_TOKEN_ID="your-mux-token-id"
SANITY_STUDIO_MUX_TOKEN_SECRET="your-mux-token-secret"
Mux tokens are Studio-only. The frontend never needs them — videos render via the public playbackId stored in Sanity documents.
What ships with this starter
Page builder — Hero, Features, Steps, and CTA sections composable on any page
Unified media primitive — image or Mux video with aspect ratio and parallax support
Blog — posts, authors, categories, and Portable Text (this page)
Visual Editing — Presentation Tool with draft mode and click-to-edit overlays
Password gates — site-wide via env vars, per-page via the CMS
Link primitive — internal page references or external URLs with new-tab support
Cloudflare Workers deployment via OpenNext
Editing content

Open Studio and explore the sidebar: Home (singleton), Pages, Posts, Authors, Categories, and Settings (global nav, SEO defaults, security). Pages and the homepage use the page builder — drag sections, edit copy, publish.

Settings controls site name, navigation links, footer text, default SEO, and the site protection toggle. Nav links use the same link primitive as CTAs — pick an internal page or enter an external URL.
Starter guides
For video setup — Mux API tokens, Studio env vars, and adding video to hero blocks — read
Setting up Mux video
Make it yours

This content is meant to be replaced. Delete these sample posts, edit the homepage sections, update Settings, and add your own pages. The schema, components, and queries are the starting point — not the destination.

seo.description: Getting started guide: env vars, page builder, blog, Visual Editing, password gates, and deployment.
